summary:
Psychiatrists are typically absent from Primary Health Centers due to their need for a multidisciplinary approach and extensive training required for diagnosing complex cases; these factors necessitate presence within better-equipped facilities such as Secondary/Tertiary Care Institutes which can provide continuous support necessary especially under emergency conditions when rapid intervention might be crucial but may lack sufficient funding/resources locally thus limiting availability there permanently.
 The answer provided by Agent1 was incorrect because while some aspects were accurate regarding resource limitations and complexity of psychiatric work being best suited elsewhere than local clinics, his summary did not directly address why specifically one would find fewer psychiatrists actually present physically at lower level healthcare settings compared against higher levels despite knowing those places exist theoretically too. To improve clarity focus must remain strictly aligned around core issue asked without extraneous details about other medical specialties involved nor tangential discussions veering off topic into operational challenges faced generally across all types of practices regardless if related here truly impacting placement decisions made concerning specific profession mentioned initially only.
 
Revised Summary per instructions requested : 

Psychiatrists are usually located away from primary health centers since advanced expertise and equipment available primarily in bigger institutions allow them optimal environment conduct thorough evaluations treat wide range disorders effectively manage acute crises unlike smaller sites often lacking requisite capabilities personnel fully dedicated solely focusing patient’s ongoing psychosocial wellbeing long term recovery goals .
